chinese_news.title: 36氪独家 | 火山引擎提升MaaS营收目标至全年150亿元，Seedance 2.0单月营收已超10亿元
chinese_news.summary:
文｜邓咏仪

编辑｜张雨忻 杨轩

36 氪独家获悉，字节跳动火山引擎在今年 4 月已将 MaaS 业务营收目标上调至 150 亿元，且这个营收目标几乎每月都在上调。而在 2025 年底，这一目标还是 100 亿元。

 36 氪了解到，在 2025 年，火山全年的 MaaS 收入约 15 亿元左右，等于截止目前 2026 年的营收目标是去年实际营收的 10 倍。可以说，火山这一波 MaaS 营收增长几乎都归功于 Seedance 2.0 的爆发——视频模型 Seedance 2.0 如今已经能带来单月超 10 亿元的收入。

一位接近火山的人士透露，截至目前，火山引擎 Token 日均消耗量仍然保持着月度近 40% 的增速。

2025 年，业界还在

chinese_news.title: 深圳具身公司星尘智能完成超10亿B轮融资，估值破百亿｜硬氪首发
chinese_news.summary:
作者｜黄楠

编辑｜袁斯来

硬氪获悉，绳驱AI机器人公司星尘智能（Astribot）近日完成B轮系列融资，三个月内三轮累计融资额超10亿元，投资方包括梁溪科创产业二期母基金（博华资本管理）、扬州龙投芯粒、中博聚力、中科创达、科德教育、某头部上市企业及国科投资等老股东持续追投。

目前星尘智能估值已突破百亿元，这也是深圳诞生的又一家具身智能百亿独角兽。此前，公司投资方阵容中已出现了腾讯、阿里、字节系机构的身影。

商业化方面，星尘智能还同步拿下了几个产业场景合作订单，包括与中科创达的千台级工业及商业服务订单并推进出海，与江都经开区共建亿元级应用创新中心落地文旅酒店场景等。

具身智能赛道竞争逻辑正发生根本性切换。行业告别侧重舞台预设演示的粗放比拼，转而直面真实物理环境下的复杂落地难题。技术路线尚未收敛，各家厂商基于对落地路径的不同理解，在传动方案、模型架构、数据策略等维度做出了差异化的探索与取舍。

星尘智能成立于2022年，创始人兼CEO来杰在AI与机器人领域拥有超过17年研发设计经

chinese_news.title: 36氪首发 | 浙大教授团队获财通、商汤投资，做高危场景具身机器人大脑
chinese_news.summary:
作者丨欧雪

编辑丨袁斯来

硬氪获悉，高危作业领域具身智能公司杭州旷行科技有限公司（以下简称“旷行科技”）近期已完成数千万元市场化第一轮融资（Pre-A轮），由财通资本和商汤国香投资。本轮资金将主要用于算法研发、产品矩阵完善及市场拓展。

旷行科技2025年成立于杭州，是一家专注为高危工业工程领域（资源矿山、能源电力、油气化工、交通城建）提供“机器人+AI大脑”运维解决方案的具身智能公司。公司创始人舒江鹏为国家级高层次青年人才、浙江大学百人计划研究员、博导，团队自2011年起开展智慧城市AI与工程机器人技术研发，拥有超过15年的行业负样本标注数据积累与技术沉淀。

不同于市面上常见的巡检机器人，旷行科技的核心能力不光是机器人在复杂工程场景下的移动控制，更多在于赋予机器人在高危作业，尤其是针对基建部分的“识别+诊断+处置”能力的“工程师大脑”。

“现在的机器人大多停留在‘巡视’阶段，能看到问题，但看不懂、更不会处置。”舒江鹏告诉硬氪。旷行科技通过自研的工程多模态大模型，融合图像、点云、超声、电磁波、

6月2日午后，香港恒生科技指数一度涨超4%。恒生科技指数成分股中，美团、腾讯控股涨近9%，阿里巴巴涨近6%。腾讯控股股价午后持续震荡上行。截至13:12，腾讯控股股价报474.4港元/股，涨幅为8.81%，总市值为4.33万亿港元。腾讯控股（00700.HK）强势拉升，收盘大涨10.46%，报481.6港元，创下2021年1月以来单日最高涨幅纪录，市值飙升至4.39万亿港元。

大公司：

高德与新加坡旅游局启动战略合作，将落地首个与海外国家旅游局共建扫街榜

36氪获悉，6月2日，高德地图与新加坡旅游局（Singapore Tourism Board）在北京签署战略合作备忘录，双方将联合打造首个海外国家旅游局共建文旅榜单——新加坡扫街榜，并将高德飞行街景技术首次落地海外。除了榜单共建，高德还将会把国内已成熟的飞行街景技术应用于新加坡，基于自研空间智能技术，以360°全景视角呈现景点、酒店、街区和餐厅实景，让游客出发前即可直观判断目的地是否符合预期。

chinese_news.title: 美团在竞争中看清自己
chinese_news.summary:
作者｜任彩茹

编辑｜乔芊

参与外卖大战的三家公司中，美团最晚发出一季度财报，但给出了最超预期的“减亏”成果。

2026年一季度，美团实现收入910亿元，同比增长5.6%，基本符合预期。亮点在于经营亏损的大幅收窄，由上季度的161亿元减至65亿元，其中最受关注的核心本地商业，经营亏损从上季度的100亿元减少到20亿元，明显好于投行预测的40-45亿元。

业绩提振下，6月2日港股开盘后，美团盘中最高涨幅超9%，截至发稿，报85.25港元/股。

值得关注的是，本季财报中美团还更新了两个关键的收入口径：

  • 一方面，将“商品销售收入”单列，主要来自食杂零售业务（以小象超市、快驴为主）以及药品、酒类等其他自营零售业务。财报称，这一变化是为了反映零售业务的战略重要性。这部分收入原本属于“其他服务及销售”，单列之后，小象超市的增长势头会更容易追踪；
  • 另一方面，将原本的“佣金收入”和“在线营销服务收入”合并，以“商家服

chinese_news.title: 豆包6��下旬正式付费，并加速打通抖音电商丨36氪独家
chinese_news.summary:
文｜肖思佳

编辑｜乔芊 杨轩

5月初旬，豆包将推出付费订阅服务的消息，引发市场广泛讨论。其在苹果App Store中更新的付费订阅方案显示，豆包将推出四档收费标准：基础版、标准版、加强版、专业版。对应的月收费价格为：免费、68元、200元、500元；年收费价格为：免费、688元、2048元、5088元。

随后官方回应：“豆包始终提供免费服务。在免费服务的基础上，我们也在探索推出更多增值内容，以满足不同用户的差异化需求。”

据36氪独家了解，以上只是豆包商业化的预热动作。在接下来的季度中，豆包将持续推进商业化的落地。知情人士透露，豆包预计将在6月下旬正式上线付费内容，并于同期举行的Force大会上更新相关功能。之所以选择这一时间节点，是因为PC端与移动端仍需约一个月时间，完成基础功能与收费体系的适配改造。

据36氪了解，若进展顺利，豆包将于三季度进一步结合电商功能更新完善付费场景，并通过补贴为抖音商城进行引流，四季度进入运行期。

chinese_news.title: 做了18年民宿的爱彼迎，开始卖酒店了？
chinese_news.summary:
爱彼迎的”进化“

五月的旧金山，正在经历一场技术的集体癫狂。硅谷的各路AI新贵忙着为机器进化摇旗呐喊，把发布会开成一场场技术狂欢，仿佛人类的下一步就是被算法接管一切。

同样的城市里，Brian Chesky——爱彼迎联合创始人兼首席执行官，站上爱彼迎旧金山总部2026夏季发布会的舞台，面对着坐满一楼中央大厅的全球媒体和围在办公大楼每层栏杆边上的爱彼迎员工，他拿应该是过去一年最让他兴奋的事开场了，不是爱彼迎如何利用AI来进化，而是——

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Bitcoin close above $70,000 on June 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: BTC fell below $66K amid US-Iran escalation; fear gauge surged 20% (biggest jump since Feb 5), signaling capitulation and potential reversal point attracting contrarian traders.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CoinDesk: Bitcoin's fear gauge surge + Cointelegraph BTC $66K breach on Iran strikes

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Ethereum outperform Bitcoin (ETH/BTC ratio >0.055) by September 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: ETH down 9% with other alts (SOL, DOGE) as crypto turns contrarian to AI stocks; Bitwise report signals ratio compression, making a mean-reversion trade attractive for traders timing oversold conditions.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: Cointelegraph: Crypto turns contrarian bet; Bitwise commentary on AI stock rotation

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will NVIDIA stock close above $140 by December 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Perplexity CEO frames AI race as 'value per watt per user'; NVIDIA's H-series dominance in efficiency is a direct play, and SoftBank's 3.1 GW AI data center commitment in France signals sustained demand for GPU capacity.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CNBC: Perplexity CEO on AI efficiency metric; CNBC: SoftBank 3.1 GW France AI data center build

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will the S&P 500 end Q3 2026 above 5,500?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: OECD cut global growth outlook citing US-Iran conflict damage; mid-May timing creates uncertainty window before summer earnings season—traders betting on whether geopolitical risk persists or fades.

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will humanoid robotics fund inflows (tracked via public ETFs/indexes) exceed $10 billion by December 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: CNBC reports investor bullishness on humanoid robot transformation; Chinese sources (36Kr: Astribot B-round >$100M valuation, Kuangxing Tech Pre-A) show explosive funding in embodied AI—Western institutional capital likely to follow.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CNBC: Investor bets on humanoid robots; 36Kr: Astribot $100B+ valuation, multiple embodied AI rounds closing

market_questions_to_create.question: Will ByteDance's Volcano Engine MaaS revenue exceed 150 billion yuan in full-year 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: ByteDance Q4 2026 earnings report or official Volcano Engine financial disclosure
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: 36Kr reported (exclusive, May 2026) that Volcano raised MaaS target from 100B (end 2025) to 150B in April 2026, with monthly targets increasing. Market has not priced in the acceleration or ByteDance's AI infrastructure momentum.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: FLAG: 36Kr exclusive—this was published before English media coverage. Signals ByteDance is outpacing OpenAI/Anthropic in cost-per-token and capturing domestic AI inference market faster than consensus expects.

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the DXY (US Dollar Index) close below 100 on August 31, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Federal Reserve H.10 daily exchange rate data (DXY closing price)
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: US-Iran conflict and OECD growth downgrade create safe-haven demand for USD, but if tariff proposals (10-12.5% on 60 economies) spark retaliation and slow US growth, dollar weakens. Current volatility offers asymmetric entry.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC: USTR tariff proposal on 60 economies; OECD slowdown forecast; geopolitical uncertainty typically supports USD but tariff escalation risks a reversal

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill market expansion reach 5 or more countries (outside US) by June 30, 2027?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Novo Nordisk press releases, investor relations filings, or regulatory approvals by country
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: CNBC reports Novo rolled out Wegovy pill in UAE (first non-US market). Market underestimates speed of geographic rollout given global obesity epidemic and healthcare infrastructure readiness in Middle East, Asia, EU.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC: Novo Wegovy pill UAE launch; signals shift from injection to pill form accelerates adoption velocity

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Alphabet's stock price exceed $210 by September 30, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Alphabet announced $80B stock buyback (unusual scale; Goldman Sachs managing deal). Large capital return signals management confidence in intrinsic value, yet market may not have fully repriced execution risk and AI revenue contribution.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC: Alphabet $80B stock sale (buyback); Goldman Sachs commentary on 'unprecedented territory' suggests volatility opportunity

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the US and Iran launch a sustained military conflict causing oil to exceed $100/barrel (WTI) by July 15, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: NYMEX WTI Crude Oil futures daily settlement price
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Current escalation (Kuwait airport damaged, drone strikes) is priced into BTC/crypto fear but oil market appears anchored below $90. If conflict widens (port blockades, refinery strikes), supply shock is acute and asymmetric upside is real.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: BBC: Kuwait airport struck; US-Iran escalation ongoing; CoinDesk: BTC fear spike but oil futures show complacency—market may be underpricing geopolitical tail risk

investment_theses.thesis: Crypto macro is in a capitulation washout; Bitcoin's fear gauge surge and 9% alt decline create a low-risk entry for 3-6 month horizon as geopolitical uncertainty fades and institutional AI infrastructure demand (SoftBank, NVIDIA, Volcano) sustains growth narrative.
supporting_signals: CoinDesk: Bitcoin's fear gauge surged 20%, biggest jump since Feb 5 crash—textbook capitulation setup
Cointelegraph: BTC below $66K on Iran strikes, but strike-driven dips are typically temporary
CNBC: SoftBank 3.1 GW AI data center build (France) + Perplexity on efficiency metrics signals sustained macro demand for GPU/blockchain infrastructure
36Kr: Volcano Engine MaaS acceleration (100B→150B YoY) reflects AI capex boom reinvigorating semiconductor/hosting sector
investment_theses.counter_view: If US-Iran war escalates into port/energy infrastructure strikes, oil spikes and stagflation re-emerges; risk-off environment extends and crypto remains a funding source (margin calls on levered positions).
investment_theses.expression: Long BTC/USD spot (buy dip at $65-67K with 2-3% position size) or BTC June 2026 perp with 3-5x leverage; hedge with short oil (WTI) or long VIX call spread if tail risk escalates

investment_theses.thesis: Humanoid robotics and embodied AI are experiencing Chinese venture capital inflection (Astribot $100B+ valuation, multiple Pre-A closures); Western institutional capital has not yet repriced the sector, creating a 4-8 month window to accumulate robotics ETF/single-name exposure before US VC and hedge funds mobilize.
supporting_signals: CNBC: 'Investors bet humanoid robots will transform industry and homes over the next decade'—mainstream bullish narrative just emerging
36Kr: Astribot B-round (100B+ valuation), Kuangxing Tech Pre-A (high-risk industrial scenarios)—Chinese startups moving to Series B/C before English media coverage
Hacker News: Modern robots still years away from replacing humans (contrarian take), but infrastructure (GPU, AI chips, funding) is accelerating development velocity
investment_theses.counter_view: Hype cycle risk: robotics has disappointed before; regulatory hurdles (labor displacement, safety certification) may slow commercialization; many startups have capital but unproven unit economics.
investment_theses.expression: ROBO ETF (iShares Robotics and AI ETF) or direct allocation to NVIDIA (structural play on robot compute demand); avoid single-name robotics startups until Series C/D or IPO clarity

investment_theses.thesis: Tariff escalation (US 10-12.5% duty on 60 economies) will compress profit margins for tech supply chains; NVIDIA, TSMC-exposed US semis (MU, QCOM), and dollar-denominated exporters face headwinds 2-4 months out, while defensive/domestic inflation beneficiaries (commodities, domestic services) outperform.
supporting_signals: CNBC: USTR proposes 10-12.5% tariffs on 60 economies for forced labor practices—politically difficult to reverse, likely to be implemented
36Kr: ByteDance/Volcano AI capex ramping globally (France, US)—tariffs will raise cost of goods for AI infrastructure vendors
CNBC: Alphabet $80B buyback signals capital confidence, but global supply chain stress may limit upside in near term
investment_theses.counter_view: Tariffs may be negotiated down or delayed; tech sector earnings resilience and margin expansion (AI pricing power) could offset headwinds; dollar strength actually benefits US-listed semis in foreign revenue terms.
investment_theses.expression: Short QQQ or overweight XLV (healthcare, defensive); long commodities (gold, oil) via GLD, USO; underweight MU, QCOM relative to SPY; long USD/CNH (dollar strength from tariff uncertainty)

investment_theses.thesis: OECD's global growth downgrade + US-Iran conflict escalation will trigger a risk-off rotation out of mega-cap tech and into short-dated Treasury yields and safe-haven assets; bond volatility and credit spreads are underpriced for a 2-3 month window before potential 'resolution' rally.
supporting_signals: CNBC/OECD: Global growth outlook cut due to Middle East conflict—first major institution to formalize slowdown scenario
BBC: US-Iran strikes escalating (Kuwait airport damaged, drone attacks)—tail risk of wider conflict is real
CoinDesk: Crypto fear gauge spike + bullish bets liquidated ($1.6B)—precursor to broader equities volatility
investment_theses.counter_view: Geopolitical conflicts often resolve faster than anticipated (ceasefire deals, diplomatic pressure); US fiscal capacity and Fed policy flexibility could mute recession fears; equity earnings resilience keeps valuations supported.
investment_theses.expression: Long TLT (20+ year Treasury ETF) or short SPY via put spreads; long VIX call spread (buy 20/25 call for ~2-3 months); underweight growth (QQQ) relative to value (VTV); hold USD strength (DXY)

investment_theses.thesis: ByteDance's Volcano Engine MaaS momentum (150B yuan target, up from 100B in 6 months) signals that Chinese open-source/frontier AI models are capturing inference market share faster than Western incumbents; this will pressure OpenAI/Anthropic pricing and threaten US AI SAAS margins 6+ months out, creating a rotation opportunity into Asia-facing AI beneficiaries and away from high-multiple US AI software names.
supporting_signals: FLAG: 36Kr exclusive (May 2026) — Volcano Engine raised MaaS target to 150B yuan, with monthly targets constantly revised up. English media has not yet registered the speed of ByteDance's AI inference dominance.
CNBC: Perplexity CEO on 'value per watt per user'—open inference models (Deepseek, ByteDance) are optimizing for this metric better than OpenAI GPT-4
36Kr: Douyin (TikTok) app introducing paid AI chat (豆包, Doubao) starting late May 2026—viral integration into 900M+ user base drives inference volume and moat
investment_theses.counter_view: OpenAI/Anthropic may maintain pricing power due to brand, API maturity, and enterprise lock-in; Chinese models face regulatory/geopolitical headwinds (US AI export controls); Volcano's 150B target may be ambitious and achievable only via low-margin consumer inference.
investment_theses.expression: Underweight OPENAI (if public), MSFT AI exposure; long Alibaba (BABA) as AI SAAS beneficiary in Asia; long HUYA/BILIBILI for exposure to AI-native short-form content monetization; consider being short high-multiple US AI SAAS (CRM, SNOW, NET) via put spreads or underweight in portfolio
